page.selectPrompt: Select a runtime to see details.
page.removeConfirm:
Remove {{id}} {{version}}?

This will delete the installation directory. This cannot be undone.
node.desc: Node.js is a JavaScript runtime built on Chrome's V8 JavaScript engine. Ideal for scalable network applications.
rust.desc: Rust is a language empowering everyone to build reliable and efficient software. Blazingly fast and memory-efficient.
python.desc: Python is a programming language that lets you work quickly and integrate systems more effectively.
go.desc: Go is an open source programming language that makes it easy to build simple, reliable, and efficient software.
java.desc: Java is a high-level, class-based, object-oriented programming language that is designed to have as few implementation dependencies as possible.
php.desc: PHP is a popular general-purpose scripting language that is especially suited to web development.
ruby.desc: Ruby is a dynamic, open source programming language with a focus on simplicity and productivity.
dotnet.desc: .NET is a free, cross-platform, open source developer platform for building many different types of applications.
bun.desc: Bun is a fast all-in-one JavaScript runtime. Bundle, transpile, install and run JavaScript & TypeScript projects.
zig.desc: Zig is a general-purpose programming language and toolchain for maintaining robust, optimal, and reusable software.
cpp.desc: C/C++ toolchain with compilers and debugger for systems programming, high-performance apps, and native libraries.
octave.desc: MATLAB-compatible environment powered by GNU Octave for numerical computing and matrix-heavy workflows.
dart.desc: Dart is a client-optimized language for building fast apps on any platform.
flutter.desc: Flutter is a UI toolkit for building natively compiled applications from a single codebase.
julia.desc: Julia is a high-performance dynamic language for technical and scientific computing.
lua.desc: Lua is a lightweight embeddable scripting language used in game engines and automation.
clisp.desc: Common Lisp environment (SBCL) for symbolic programming and advanced macro systems.
r.desc: R is a language and environment for statistical computing and graphics, widely used in data science and research.
view.noVersionsFound: Could not load versions. Use Refresh or try again later.
wizConfig.title: Installation Settings
wizConfig.desc: Choose how you want to install {{name}} on your system.
wizConfig.systemOnlyTip: {{name}} is installed from your distribution repositories (DNF, APT, or Pacman). Isolated local install is not available for this runtime—use System Package Manager below.
wizConfig.systemOnlySkipDeps: The dependencies step is skipped. Optional tools like Composer are not required for a distro package install.
wizConfig.systemDesc: DNF / APT with fixed distro package names—the version dropdown does not change what the OS installs.
wizConfig.localDesc: User-scope install without sudo where supported; Node, Python, and Go use the version you picked.
wizConfig.refreshTitle: Refresh version list from the network (new releases appear here without an app update)
wizConfig.apiNote: Lists are loaded from upstream APIs where possible; use Refresh after a new release if you do not change language.
wizConfig.systemNote: System list shows only Java versions currently available from your distro repositories.
wizConfig.systemModeNote: System mode now shows only repository-backed choices for this runtime on your distro.
wizConfig.methodNote: With System, the target version shown above is informational only for most languages—we call fixed package names (nodejs, etc.). Pick Local for Node.js, Python, or Go if you want the selected version installed.
wizConfig.addToPathDesc: Automatically configure environment variables for this runtime.
wizConfig.sudoDesc: For system installs (dnf / apt / snap): paste your sudo password here, or leave blank — a graphical password prompt (pkexec) usually opens. Leave blank too if passwordless sudo is enabled.
wizDeps.title: System Dependencies
wizDeps.desc: We found the following requirements for building/running {{name}}.
wizDeps.headerNote: 💡 Note: Some missing headers might be required for building.
wizProgress.title: {{action}} {{name}}
wizProgress.pleaseWaitInstall: Please wait while we set up your environment...
wizProgress.pleaseWaitUpdate: Please wait while we update runtime packages and verify the version...
wizProgress.pleaseWaitRemove: Please wait while we remove runtime files and clean shared dependencies safely...
wizProgress.jobOk: ✔ Job finished. Check VERIFY lines below if present.
wizProgress.jobWarn: Job finished but post-install VERIFY reported a problem—see log.
wizFinish.reviewLog: Review step 3 job log—the VERIFY lines show what a login shell could find on PATH.
wizFinish.reviewLogSystemOnly: Review the installation log above—the VERIFY lines show what a login shell could find on PATH.
wizFinish.verifyOk: Post-install check reported a toolchain on PATH.
wizFinish.verifyRetry: No VERIFY OK line yet—retry after opening a new terminal.
wizFinish.verifyFail: VERIFY failed—the binary was not visible on PATH in a login shell.
wizFinish.systemNote: You used System; the distro may have only confirmed packages already on disk. Choose Local next time if you want the version you picked in the dropdown.
wizFinish.stepVerify: • Try running {{cmd}} in a new terminal
uninstall.title: Confirm Remove {{name}}
uninstall.desc: Choose how much cleanup to apply. Preview reflects detected distro and package mapping.
uninstall.noPackages: No package-managed items detected for this runtime.
uninstall.sharedDeps: Shared dependencies will NOT be removed (used by other runtimes): {{deps}} Runtime removal will continue safely without deleting them.
